  • Cyber Attacks

    Cyber attacks

    In this chapter we have discussed computer hacking. These are those malicious attacks carried out in a way to steal, modify or destroy data. It also includes all forms of computer and cyber viruses. In detail we have covered:

      1. How to Protect Yourself from All Types of Attacks
    A cyber-attack can be carried out by a number of sources including but generally speaking it falls into several categories:
        • Computer attacks: Attacks on computers and computer networks are generally carried out on devices, like laptops, desktop computers and mobile devices. This is the most prevalent form of attacks. These attacks can be carried out by various methods like phishing, click frauds, Trojan horse, social engineering attacks, etc. The targets here are mostly computers, but there are various other methods that can be used for similar purposes.
        • Phishing & Recaptcha scams: These attacks are usually carried out via different methods like emails, instant messaging, etc. Phishing attacks are aimed at tricking the target users into disclosing their personal information that include usernames and passwords. These attacks are carried out in a large scale and can be very hazardous both for you and the victim.

          Recaptcha, often known as an old school email scam and can be carried out by using various methods. It is a widely recommended password and also used for various websites as you can see here

          What is the best 1 step password for iPhone?

          You can try the following 1-step password suggestions on different sites: https://www.iconsimality.com/default.png 1+password, https://www.ip.be/1+password, https://www.ip.be/2+password, https://www.iconsimality.com/1+password+2, https://www.iconsimality.com/1+password+3, https://www.iconsimality.com/1+password+4
          If you don’t know these 1-step passwords or don’t have time to check all popular sites, you can use the 3-step password suggestions below.
          So if you want to get the password to a website, only for a specific domain, how many-step-passwords are there? If you use a three-step password suggestion below, you will not only avoid getting registered to the wrong site by checking, but also avoid having to spend money to get access to some or all of your data.
          It is clear that there are more to choose a password than just the password with 1-step suggestions. It can be either at least simple characters, as explained after the code below, or one of the 3-step passwords suggestions below.

          When you create a new account, the first time you enter your password you must provide a unique two-word password like your email, which is an example of a simple-to-understand password. Each subsequent registration is then based on the second two-word password you entered in the first registration.

          This way you can create an account with just one password — and it's easy to remember and memorize each time you log in. This is a big help if you ever need to log in again and cannot remember your account number.

          Password Guidelines


          A hacker is unlikely to use a hard-to-remember password like your email and it's unlikely that he will try your social media accounts. But a better approach is to create passwords that are easy to remember but difficult to crack. These password combinations should work when you're not looking at them in your browser, such as when you're driving, shopping, or doing research. However, if you need to access the internet while driving, it's best to use a longer password and make it password-protected.


          Use strong passwords that are at least 15 characters long, including numbers, letters, and punctuation. Consider the character count of a common phrase you are entering, such as "I am going out for breakfast today" (in a Google search this could be a 26 plus digit password).


          Use one or more complicated words. If you can't think of a sentence, think of an alternative. For example: "The moon" or "a great big love letter" (two common words for "moon") are both strong passwords. Don't use common phrases, such as "my friend" or "me" (this one, too). If you don't know this person - or a spouse or friend - don't write your password down.

      Note: You can store data or write passwords onto paper disks with an on-page padlock application.
      The easiest ways to ensure password strength are to not store data or write passwords onto paper disks with an on-page padlock application. In a document you have to type out the password, then the padlock application to secure it, and finally the paper padlock in a lockbox. This is a quick and easy process.
      (Note: There is a "Padlock-Lock" service)

  21. What is the role of a password cracker in your organization?

    One of the primary targets of Password Cracker Attacks is to make a dictionary full of dictionaries, and to try all of those dictionaries until you pick the one that's the most likely to match your current password. This can happen when your security staff tries several different passwords for the same object, or they crack a few different passwords for a specific user account.
    I will let you know more about this in another upcoming article, so stay tuned.

    Hope you are enjoying these password cracking tricks.


  • 1 "A"

    How does this work?

    I was just able to get a little insight to how this works, but the original problem was just to crack at least one password.

    I did this on an older Windows Vista machine, using a regular backup. It took 10 minutes to get the file in the proper place, the second backup took about 5 minutes, and the backup was restored in a few days with a second backup in a year or so.

    From what I've seen this method only works if your password is a single case word (just some random "a" to "e" is good). I can still remember some password I can remember that was not in that list.

    This doesn't work on any of my machines that use a password change feature, including my laptop.


  • Hint.


    That won't work on Microsoft's Active Directory systems.

    If you need multiple backups, the first backup can be a "standard backup" of the current password. The second backup can be a backup of your current PasswordChangeLog, the third backup can be an incremental backup of the password (if you're using active directory). And the fourth backup can be a backup of the password itself.

    Finally, if you're using a Microsoft product, then the third backup is the latest and greatest: it's likely the latest and greatest backup that everyone uses when they install Windows. Just be sure to make all of the backups that have different passwords on each disk, and make an effort to avoid keeping just some of the passwords in memory.

    For the most part, I will try to recommend users to look into setting up a hard drive encrypted password. For the most part, these passwords are still easy to crack, and they usually do not have any information of the data on the hard drive that you wish to keep secure.

  6. The following applies to encryption. It does not apply to data encryption (except if you are sending information with a secure protocol). You will want to understand encryption so you can choose a good method.

   Author's note: This post was submitted for the author's own evaluation. It can be considered a "test subject", or a submission without the author's written permission.

Weak Encryption

You should have guessed it, the password is a weak encryption. The weakest thing is simply that there isn't any secure method that works flawlessly. For passwords of all types, the strongest is the system you find in a computer database. A computer password is like a code. It's not something you can remember. Instead it's something that you only use out of the box. If you have to remember a large number of secret codes, it's much better to use a system that is hard to forge.

SHA-1 (hash) encryption (sometimes called sha-2, sha-1, sha-12 or 256) and its variants do not allow us to check whether a password was passed down incorrectly.

So when you enter a password, it will show up as sha-1 on the screen. You can verify the password by clicking on the bottom of the screen, which tells someone that the pass-phrase is a sha-1 hashed version of the real password.


To validate someone's password you can compare the pass-phrase from the question to the one on the screen and verify that it matches. There are tools available online for that purpose, but there are also sites where you can see if you did it right. (You may like them, but they are slow to load and don't have a feature I know about. If you want them...).


If this doesn't solve your password problem you might want to try using a password manager. They store all your passwords in a way that makes them easy to remember. The problem, of course, is that it's a lot of work to track down every password. A free online one is available here. (I'm guessing your login does not require it to do it). Another is password-based security. It's good though because it doesn't need you to remember passwords for every site you log on to. See this page for a lot more than that. It might be worth checking out if and to expand your knowledge further.

I think the key to really great password management is to be as good as possible. Not so easily said, yet quite true and effective. So a good place to start in that realm, is reading the tips on those password suggestions and reading up on the topic itself. The first one for me is "The secret to better passwords is to do word play". That's right, actually playing with letters and numbers. No. it's more or less typing in each word "on the top of the stack" and making them look random. In a bad password, that means you'll end up with a big, hard word for a few characters. The harder to guess it is a combination, the easier to guess it gets. The trick is making a couple of words rhyme so when you do them, you'll have a single set of words, well that's right, you've changed my password. Once you've the new set, you just have to replace those letters one by one. For example, instead of replacing "F", for "F!" with "A.", I'll do it like this.

So, what's a mix between the number 4 and the word A? Or 6 with your new set of letters:

(2,4,6,6)
(4,6,4,6)

(2,4,6,4,6)

(4,6,2,6)

(2,4,6,6,6)

(4,6,6,6)
(2,4,6,4,6)

(2,4,4,6,6)
(4,6,2,6,4)
(2,4,6,6,4,2)

(2,4,5,6,6,4), for example. All you need to do for me now is have a little idea - of where on a keyboard to click to get the letters right. I want to write 'f' as 5, so I'll take this 'f' and have '6' as 4, and move down to '6,'. It seems a bit more logical now. Let's try 'f' as A. I've already got 4,5 as 4, so now 2, as 6, which is 2,6,6,6 but not 6, or A,5, as I start typing. I'm a little bit late. No, no, no. And this process can be repeated forever. Just start with capital letters such as A or 3. Just do that. And repeat it as many times that you see fit.

Well, we've just got 2 choices: A,3,1,4,7 or F,a,1,5,6. Then again, as always, once you've got those few vowels right, then you can move on. The trick, of course, is to go very near the 'fricatives' and 'breathy consonants'' to get over to them. So if I type 'f,f,f,f' (it's in square brackets) I can go through the whole formula for any consonant sound. You can just make it easy.

So you've probably figured out enough to be in a hurry. We can do the next part of the problem and it's a matter of going through the 'dental fricatives' and 'lateral fricatives'' - the latter being just those friceds that happen to go between the 'th' and /l/ (that's the 'w' as in 'word' or 'weigh' which will be a bit more hard to manage for a while).

If you have a 'hard' l and you end up on the 'hard' 't', then you haven't made the 't' the first sound in that letter - it's a sound change. That's the best part of 'lateral fricatives'! They don't do it all the time, usually a sudden change.

I've just taken a look at my writing, and you'll see I'm a bit more relaxed now. The 300 characters I wrote today is a lot more relaxed than I was when I started! It's important to pay attention to the level of your writing, as your l's, r's etc really don't do all that much there.

If I had been able to record that I had, I'd probably have a slightly worse start. The letter f, I'd be a wee bit bit further out of the normal writing range.

So, if you've been making up letters all day, try to think through the following.

Use your most relaxed letters the best to make your writing faster but not necessarily to make your writing harder. Be aware that you'll probably need to use all of your most relaxed letter sounds and combinations on your words. In order to speed the development of your most relaxed letter sounds, you'll probably need to spend at least two hours on those most relaxed letter sounds without your writing becoming more difficult. This isn't a recipe for failure, but it won't be the best idea.

You also need to be aware that some sounds are better with some letter combinations than others. Certain sounds come into the language just naturally when they're written in certain combinations. "H" is one such sound, which makes one difficult letter. You can teach that sound from scratch, but usually it's easier to find a word or sentence in which the word comes into a certain combination like this:

  • hh
  • hee
  • heehee 

Your brain automatically knows the sound the hh sound makes and you'll find that if you're trying to write a sentence that uses the hh sound without knowing a word, you'll almost instantly misprint it. It needs to find a word and use a sound that naturally fits with that word.

When you're writing text, the best way to break it down is to write a block of lines until you feel confident with the character you're working with. Let your brain know what it hears in the first few lines, and you may find that as you're writing the rest, your brain starts coming up with more ideas that sound good to you.

Another good sign? You may notice in a sentence that you're getting things right.

When you feel confident with your speech, take a break. You may find that you are more open and your brain has time to think about the thoughts behind the way you want people to vote. Even taking a break is important, so try to be honest about when you are feeling like you got everything figured out. Once you feel more confident with your ideas and you can start writing, take each new idea and brainstorm. When you're ready to create, read everything you've just written or look through any other ideas and see if you can come up with a few.

Finally, when going to the polls, have faith that people won't vote that you don't. It might be hard to know what to expect so you'll want to do the right thing by not just saying they can't vote. You might ask a friend for advice. Ask your boss if you can do some consulting.

To make sure you come through, don't forget to be confident and proud of what you're doing.

Catching the President of the U.S. Presidential Election

At the end of November, the election is likely between the presidential candidate who received the most votes and the candidate who receives the lowest number of votes. That means that it's possible that the winner of that election is either 70 people - the winner of the National Popular Vote list (NPV)(see below) or 39 people - both winners of the National Popular Vote list (NPV) - and so it's also possible that the winner of those two lists will contest the election for president, but not necessarily take office for that very reason.

The most likely outcome of the National Popular Vote effort is that a candidate will be chosen by more than the total of those eligible to vote. So with the NPV, it's possible that a candidate is eligible to vote for president but won't take office because of who their opponent is.

As for presidential elections, the National Popular Vote petition to the United States Congress has some interesting problems. For one thing, there are no requirements for states, so there are no guarantees of where a candidate's electoral votes will come from.

In addition, the United States has different rules for dividing electoral votes between presidential candidates, meaning that the percentage of each state's vote must be more than 1/3 of a percentage point below the total Electoral College votes needed to win a election. The results, however, are that the Democrats, who lost two states with large margins of victory in the election, won one-third of those states for a margin of less than 1/4 of a percentage point. The margin was 31 and 33, respectively, in the Electoral College. The Republicans won five states with big margins of victory and two major win states from Illinois, Arkansas, and Florida, where they could comfortably claim 50.7 percent of the popular vote and 37 state votes. The Democratic state of Ohio was 44 percent Democratic and 36 percent Republican. The Democratic state of Connecticut, which had the lowest percentage of electoral votes, qualified as a swing state (with a two-forver 53 percent difference).

On Election night last night, the Republican-controlled House of Representatives held the first-ever 24-hour session in the Electoral College. On election night, the clock struck midnight, when the Republicans won by 288 votes; but the Republicans won by only a hundred votes. The House Democrats took control on the night of the election, and the Democrats lost their majority in the House for the second time this year.

The Republicans lost control of the House on Thursday, when only Democrats took control.

On Tuesday, the Democrats had led Republicans in the Senate by a little over a million votes. The Democrats gained control on Tuesday by a little over a million votes.

So, when the Republicans won the House, then who got the Senate? The Democrats, again.

The Republicans won the Senate. Democrats, the Democrats won the House. The House Democrats lost. So, the Republicans lost.


So, Republicans have come up with a political strategy. The Democrats didn’t have it and they tried to replace Republican ideas with Republican ideas, and that’s why the Republicans lost.

So Congress needs to follow the same rule with immigration reform.

Look, the idea is simple. If you leave all the country, there should be no immigration problems. If you take a nation, we’re going to help it. If you take a nationality and you take a people, we’re going to help that person. We’re not going to let those people off the boat.

So why aren’t we doing that?

The reason, of course, is the reality that the Congress is controlled by big business, big income groups, big religion, big military-intelligence. They’re in control and therefore, they will fight to keep the people away from the solution.

The solution would be a law that would make it illegal to send funds to the Muslim community to have them destroyed, or to send military forces to attack them, or to create new mosques or neighborhoods, or whatever those are.


The Muslims are the only ones in this country whose rights are being violated, because the Muslim community is the only one that is in control of the State.

And we need this law. This law must be enacted by the Legislative Branch of this government so that everyone’s voice is heard in this struggle for justice that is taking place. This must be implemented and it must be enforced for generations to come, and we will pay for that. We are in the process of putting this Law into effect.  We will pay a little money to give to them. This is our goal. You need this Law. And we need other support from individuals and NGOs to help us create this law. It will be much bigger, much more powerful, very much a more comprehensive proposal.

The Law shall be enacted by Law for the people of Pakistan to be recognized and recognized by the people of India, Australia, Singapore and several other countries.
Our Government will make this law for us to sign it. When this law is enacted and all the members and the NGOs agree to its implementation, then our Government will enforce it on all the people of Kashmir, whether they are armed from the jungle, the mountains or armed, not out of any fear, but out of the hope of making peace with Kashmir. We shall see what the result will be.
The Government of India can not break out of the law unless it was signed by the Government of Pakistan in the process. When the law was put in the Parliament, Pakistan could not do anything unless this law was signed. Pakistan is the only country that stands in the court of law. They cannot break it without their own consent. They made up and signed something on their own without any approval of Congress, and now they have to have their permission. I have to say this, if Pakistan Government passes Law and this is signed by Parliament or General Council/Parliament or President then Pakistan has to abide by the Law and do this. Otherwise this cannot be done for example they have to agree Pakistan to pay BPO bills, if the Government cannot agree to it then it must have its own approval, they have to agree to BPO Bill in Parliament and cannot stop it. In Pakistan there are so many laws like land and security laws for example there are security laws like security of life, safety in general, security against illegal things. If they don’t agree to the security laws then the security laws cannot be enforced by the government force. And I agree there is also security police of Pakistan and those soldiers who come in the province of Pakistan and they have good intelligence, they have to come in the province of Pakistan so that they can take those law to force the public to see the law is actually written in the law. And that’s the law and we would have to give the police force, the police force the time to enforce such laws and in our country, for example, the police force can only stand up against a citizen who does not believe in freedom, or who does not wish to enforce the law. Because if he wants to go to jail or to prison or to give some other punishment against his friends and people in jail or in prison. He can simply ask the other party in front of the court, who will not know why, or maybe because he was accused of something already.

And in fact I think that the only way to overcome the opposition or in general the anti-freedom or anti-humanity of a country of this kind is to ban people and institutions that do not comply with the human beings demands to do otherwise or to fight and defend the human-based rights, because the government of the country who is the ruling state has already given up its human rights. This means that there are only two possibilities at this moment. The first is the human right, and the second is the freedom or human nature of society.

Some historians and sociologists have taken advantage of the new economic boom to say that the 1990s were a period of “economic stagnation” because the economic crisis that they attributed to the bad banking system in the 1980s was “economic boom.” The only alternative to the “bad banking system” is the “good banking system.” This approach is just an excuse for not getting a grip on the money that the great recession created.

But the great economic recession was an experiment. People who wanted to borrow money at good rates were willing to borrow it at, say, 8% interest. And when the banks went belly up, those borrowers stopped borrowing. This was because borrowers were being offered so much interest that they wanted it even if they actually couldn’t pay back the loan. This was an experiment, even though it produced winners and losers, winners because people were willing to buy houses and make mortgage payments, and losers because they lost their home, because they were not able to pay their home. This was not a market failure, but a market failure to get a certain return on the mortgage, and so the banks had the market price.

The experiment also failed to provide a sufficient means to make a rational decision about the amount of money spent on housing. The reason is that the banks had a financial system that did not exist. They had an unorganized monopoly and could provide no guarantees that they would have to. The government made it possible for them to provide guarantees. With these guarantees people could buy houses, and then lend money for mortgages whenever they wanted to do so.

The government then provided a means to make an intelligent decision as to what to do with the money that was not being used to make sure the system of property was running smoothly and that every person who wanted to live in the nation (except for the government) understood that it was being provided and not having to buy a house!

These were the conditions in which American businessmen could get away with taking loans from other companies with the idea that they would later repay them as they had not been paid; the means for the repayment being a check which was drawn by the debtor as the check was signed so that the money would be given in advance to repay in case of default. The system of giving the loan in advance was very profitable to the bankers.

It was the same with the banks. When banks took credit loans, when they refused to lend if they did not have the money to satisfy the borrower, they were not doing nothing but giving bankers money as collateral, either by lending money or by promising to lend money to lenders. When banks took credit loans, when they refused to lend if they did not have the money to satisfy the borrower, they also were giving depositors money as collateral.

What happened? Banks did not want to accept the risk that depositors would demand even more collateral when they were served. Banks knew that they could only get the funds they needed as hard currency. The only bank who could make a profit as a lender of last resort was the bank that did not accept the depositors' collateral.
What is done by the bank that is not an alternative lender

The first step of these banks was to print money as their funds were being given to the depositors. These banks did not lend or borrow to the depositors. Instead, they lent out to depositors the paper that represented new currency. As they would lend in exchange for their money they would transfer the old money from one depositor to another. At the same time, the new money was also being given to the first bank because they did not expect anyone to find any other way to get the new money. The second step was to convert the paper currency to bank deposits. The paper currency was given to banks as loans to help meet the balance.
In other words, banks lend and borrowers take deposits in the form of new money and it takes three steps to get the new, centralised money out into the economy. A depositary deposit helps a bank to lend new money out to other banks which in turn lend new money out to other banks and so on. By the central banks, banks, and the private businesses as a whole, we should all be able to get as much new money as they can and also as much to loan if we have to borrow. We could be allowed to invest as we like, we could invest as we like as our banks and businesses will be allowed to run as they like. The market economy and the financial world would not even be run as it is in any other way. It would be entirely a free market economy.”

The argument is that there is no room for government intervention. There can be no private sector, and all the money would come in on the backs of firms and workers who are trying to produce as much as they can. If we go completely private, we have too much. To make this vision possible, we must create a new tax system with government only doing what our private sector and governments cannot.

The argument for a return to private property is also obvious. If property is private, as it should be, we will be more self-supporting. When property comes into the marketplace, owners will have more to spend, and less will be left over for other people. That reduces overall consumption and prices, and allows for more efficient trade and economies of scale. If more is spent than not, then profits keep increasing at the expense of less so that resources can be used efficiently. If all resources were privately owned and used optimally, there would be less wealth destruction. Private property is not about efficiency or fairness. It’s about maximizing economic profits—not about avoiding or minimizing others’ pain or suffering.

I am not alone in seeing this point. For example, there is a study on how private capital has affected global oil prices and market prices, and much of the criticism of the study can be summed up in the fact that most of the analysis is based on profit sharing, rather than on the truth (which is true).


Why should people be troubled that there are not enough “levers” like capital out there to stop or reverse the world’s climate catastrophe?

First and foremost, a new study by the World Policy Institute (WPI) suggests that: 

“Climate change is a crisis of too much capital. There is a real crisis around the core of this crisis that’s coming from the capital itself. The fossil fuel corporations have grown exponentially, and they have reached a point of no return” (page 11).

The WPI’s research shows that: 

The World Bank’s new estimate of the climate crisis predicts that the only countries with the maximum amount of human capital will survive (Page 8, 11). This is based on predictions of three main characteristics: high investment in research and development, high investment in the transition, and a high level of economic growth (Page 7). 

Most countries are getting poorer, not wealthier. While more education means the opportunity for furthering their own development, their access to technology also limits their ability to make their own resources work and improve the quality of life. 

A third aspect that is underlining the lessons of the last few decades is one that applies to the US and other developed world: how the rich are using their wealth. As a rule of thumb, it's not the rich who get richer, it's the poor. This is particularly stark because of things like predatory lending and the lack of financial regulation (read the NBCC here).  

The final lesson that's coming is that it is really time for an actual shift in attitudes. There is a real possibility that we will see the world becoming a much more open and open place. If people realize that there is room for them, then they will take it. It will take time, and I am not sure if we will see a huge, sudden shift, but I can't imagine that happening.

- - - -

This was a fairly comprehensive week I hope.

My question to all the readers is - is this a turning point? Is it not possible for us to have a real, active shift that will cause serious changes in the way we think about our place together? Is this a possibility which is yet to be realised?

If not, then I hope to be right next week

I believe that we are headed a step in the right direction, with the idea of an open government which will work for a variety of sectors - from healthcare, education, entertainment, education, agriculture, defence.

The idea is simply to make sure there are institutions and processes that will ensure that the processes are based on transparency, accountability, access to information and the right to consultation.

A key part of that is making sure that there is transparency within and across government - this transparency being in terms of how data is stored and shared, as well as access to services, how the governments can work with stakeholders and individuals, and the ability to access information.

And then there's need for the Government of Ireland to be independent from outside interests - this is the key role of the Government, so the Government in its own right, should be able to make decisions without interference from an outside interest.

 For example, it is the role of the Government as a member of the European Union to have the power to apply the European Trading Rules (ETS) regarding trade. And if you are in Ireland, you will receive the same protection in this treaty in the EU, so it's a duty to be independent from outside interests.

 The role, of course, of the European Council is that it is the final authority in the day to day administration of the treaties or agreements between the countries.

The ETS is a special treaty of the ETSs of the EU and the World Trade Organisation. And the ETSs or trading agreements are the rules that the member states have to adhere to, so that these trade agreements could be made. They would basically be trading arrangements, but in practice these are agreements between companies that are involved with different areas in the various parts of the world, such as shipping or energy, transport of goods, environmental policy, security or climate-change policy, to provide some of the common measures that are agreed between the member states.


It is important to be aware of where these ETSs and trading agreements are to ensure that they do not allow countries to take advantage of opportunities provided by the ETSs or to benefit from the ETSs and trading agreements but not to be able to exploit them and to avoid the impact of the ETSs and the trading agreements.

Exchange rates


The European Commission aims to maintain a favourable exchange rate environment for the adoption of the ETSs or the trading agreements with other countries and this objective is achieved by having a fair and equitable exchange rate.


The European Commission’s objective is to maintain a favourable exchange rate environment for the adoption of the ETSs or the trading agreements with other countries. The purpose of these two ETSs is to ensure that the Euro continues to remain the base currency of international trade.


The European Commission’s objective is that the Euro remains the base currency to which its Member States undertake to exchange their domestic products in the exchange market at the level of the ETSs or the trading agreements. The target exchange rate in each Member State is determined by the following factors: the level of its domestic output; the comparative advantages of the Member State: the exports of its major sector; the value and comparative efficiency of the service sector; and the real interest rates in each Member State. In practice, each Member State often determines the exchange rate in conjunction with other factors. The EU countries all follow the recommendations of the International Comparison Programme Inter-State Trade and Exchange Rate Policy (ITPRP). The ITPRP report provides a summary of some of the major issues in the EU economy and sets the framework for EU trade policy. In 2014, the 27 member states held a referendum that voted in favour of leaving the EU. The decision came as expected by most international trade experts, but not by the EU’s political representatives. In the case of the EU, the position of the EU as a member state was a key issue that was questioned by the EU’s own economic policy.

The EU countries also agreed on a common monetary policy that was similar to the Euro currency exchange rate system. In 2014, the EU used the euro currency exchange rate system to deal with price fluctuation of its own economies. However, this happened after the UK and Ireland joined the EU after 30 years.

The main aim of the EU’s economic policies was to balance the EU’s fiscal imbalances for better cooperation between the member countries. The goal was achieved after the 1970s economic crisis, which is also the same as the present economic crisis. However, even with an agreement between eurozone members, the crisis is a hard fight. And the main aim of the eurozone countries is to maintain their currency’s strength by reducing the level of debt.

The main causes of problems have been to reduce inflation, reduce trade deficits and lower the level of unemployment. These are all related to the lack of adequate regulation for the trade between the countries, and with no fiscal policy, not many people can manage. The central bank of eurozone can’t control any issue in the world.


The 2010-11 financial crisis is the most important of the 2009-11 crisis and was started by the failure of banks in Greece and Italy to pay their debts to the international banks. The 2010-11 crisis is linked by economic growth, and has not happened in 20 years due to the failure of the eurozone because of the political crisis and not economy. What the 2009-11 recession did, was to destroy the banks and have a huge shock to credit market. In the first part of the year 2010, the market stopped the economy and so not all the loans and mortgages were for consumption. The 1985-1991 recession was caused by the fact that the demand of housing and mortgages went by the board and not from the buyers. The rise of debt by some bank customers led to a credit crisis and the bank customers had a big shock and were reluctant to spend as this was not guaranteed by them for the future.

The crisis started to explode in the 2010, caused by some bankers. The reason was the banks were paying excessive interest because they had no longer used their reserves. The banks were also having trouble making new loans. The situation was worsening and the credit crisis created an enormous problem for the economy.

To understand how mortgage defaulting happened one needs to look back to the 1890s. There have always been people who had no place in the society. Many of them were not productive as they did not have a proper education because they had never been educated. As a result, they had no place in society either. The middle class was always inferior to the other two. This created a sense of insecurity. However, the middle class was in a very privileged position. They could buy many things and had a good house and a proper place to live. The middle class was the most desirable class in society as they could be assured that they would have enough money to satisfy their needs. However, they rarely had more than $1000 in their pockets before they even received their education. They never knew where they were going. This was very disheartening to them as they long ago forgot their feelings and were becoming more confident in their talents and abilities and they felt more and more at ease and confident.
Furthermore, their economic status often influenced their ability to pay their rent or mortgage. For example, the poor class would often have to sell everything because their income did not cover their everyday expenses. It was also a common trait of poverty that the poor could not carry their own goods and that was often done by being in debt as well. They often had no way to acquire money, which also influenced their behavior.

2- Living on the street
Living on the street was a common experience for many poor families. Sometimes, the elderly and young children would not have enough to eat and they would run to the street for anything. They were often hungry as they did not have anything to eat. People who lived in poverty often lived on the streets, either as homeless people or those who did not have homes. The city was full of homeless people and children. 94% of people who had no money went on the streets. These people, including the elderly, lived on the streets as beggars. Their living conditions were not better than the ones in the streets. One study also reported that people who lived on the streets worked for an average of 26 weeks out of seven (25-30). People who became homeless before the war continued to work long hours in the early 1940's. They were not allowed to work any more in the city. Another study reported that 19 of the 100 young workers who began to work for the army were forced off the street. They were not allowed to work in areas where they thought they should work.
The conditions of working in a city were poor. For example, one study reported that the average work day was less than 4.5 hours. It is estimated that approximately five million people, including 8 million women, worked under this poor "conditions." There was no opportunity or freedom for those who chose to leave their jobs or were unable to leave "because of illness, disability, or the law."
In some cities only certain groups of families had free choice over where they wanted to live and work. In other cities, employers could only hire families with a certain amount of money and with their children attending school.
On average, workers in informal cities were paid in cash; in places where goods were produced, they were paid in goods and services. Workers also had free access to public facilities such as libraries, parks, and museums. This was a significant step in making public spaces accessible to the greater part of the population.
Many African cities, particularly those in West or Central Africa, had large numbers of poor and marginalized people. On occasion, urban centres functioned as places of work force concentration, particularly cities where people are often concentrated in urban poverty. There was a lack of space and accommodations to support the increased demands of urban dwellers on basic needs. Most of the rural population in countries such as Kenya, Kenya, Mozambique, Tanzania, and southern Cameroon lived in camps and rural housing developments. In order to improve housing conditions, these cities used the government's land to set up temporary permanent housing projects. Many did not have a high standard of living and some had inadequate living conditions. The poor quality of cities' construction and maintenance programs contributed to the development of urban slums.

Social Issues

In many African cities, the quality of life was a social issue. The social conflicts over housing and employment led to riots and violence. Some conflicts were long.

The major problems facing Africans living in Africa's cities were their lack of access to information, education, and basic health services and infrastructure, poor sanitation, poverty and health, lack of job opportunities, inadequate housing and employment, violence, inadequate social programs, and poor welfare. The people suffered from violence, which was mostly from bandits and organized crime groups in order to survive and maintain their wealth.

In the late 1800s, to begin with, slavery had not been abolished in Nigeria but had been legalized under British ownership as a result of the United Kingdom taking over Nigeria under its influence of the Biafran war. It is believed that 80% of the inhabitants of the rural areas of Niger lived in areas not officially declared to be “African” even though the majority of the inhabitants live in the cities though the majority of the urban population were Muslims and converted to the main religion of the country at a much lower rate than the majority of Christians, primarily of Christianity. The majority of the rural population was Sunni Muslims who believed the Quran could be converted to their language but had no direct contact with Islam except within their religious community. A majority of the villages in Niger were Muslim. As a result some villages were in Muslim hands while other villages were uncompromisingly Christian. If you looked around you might find a lot of churches in these villages and many had Arabic and Islamic inscriptions on them. If you were lucky it was possible to get a translation of the Qur'an. There were no Muslim rulers in 1902 as a result of the Niger-Congo migration. It took more than a century for a Christian king, Charles Lamar,to make Nigeria a kingdom and one which the United States recognized as a nation.

After Niger became independent in 1960, Nigeria became divided up into a number of states. It has never been called a nation of people on terms of nationality. Each of the state capitals were carved out of the country for political convenience.

Before Nigeria was divided into States, a large region was left out where the people were not recognized by the state borders. These places were the areas were the British did not recognize the boundaries of Southern Nigeria, but they recognized the borders of Northern Nigeria. Thus, Southern Nigeria, comprising the present day regions of the states of Sokoto, Kano and Ejico, became an independent area of the country. The border of Northern Nigeria and the United States was later recognized by the government of the United States.

The Białowie ?s conflict became a point of contention between the Nigerian government and the ethnic groups of the Białowie ?s. The conflict was between the Białowie ?s, or Southern Nigeria, and the Bakassi, or Northern Nigeria, and an estimated 400 Białowie ?s who had originally joined the FDI in the late 1980  s began to migrate from Nigeria to the Białowie ?s. Some of them migrated as refugees; some became traders.

In the northernmost region, 126 people (roughly 80 percent of the total number of refugees) were found in the B&B area. However, most of these were of Congolese descent, as the former Congolese provinces where they grew up also were in the north. Their language group was the Sandawe (an extinct Ndebele language of southern and western Africa; this language has been replaced by English), and they were found primarily in B?n?s.


From the beginning to the present, the GBI has maintained a relatively stable policy of maintaining only a small number of Tutsi refugees, as most of the refugees had already fled over the frontier in search of more permanent homes. However, the GBI recognized a large number of refugees from the former Mbuti to the north who had come under heavy attack by the government, and, through its cooperation with the UPDF (now the Rwandan Patriotic Front), a major effort was made by this group, as well as for a large number of Tutsi refugees, to establish the GBI. By early 1993, the number of refugees from the Rwanda that left the country and from the Democratic Republic of the Congo had reached 20 percent and was rapidly increasing, in spite of the lack of access for UNHCR and other humanitarian agencies to deliver them. According to UNRWA, the number reaching a 15 percent or higher evacuation in that month has been reduced to 45 percent between November and March. According to UNHCR, the UNDP was forced to intervene to take care of the refugees and to deal with the refugee issue. In spite of the lack of access, UNRWA, and the UNDP, continues to be at the head of the Refugee policy in the World Conference on Refugees. UNRWA supports the UNDP in the Refugee policy and provides assistance to the UNDP in case they face a challenge and require assistance from the UN agencies.

Some of the conflicts and threats in Africa are mainly the conflicts and threats that happened in the past century. As many of these wars were started in Africa, the first conflicts in Africa emerged from the past, which could be said to be the most important crises in the world today.

The wars started in Africa

The first conflicts in Africa can be traced from the 1800’s. These wars were the reasons for the first independence of Africa. There are major conflicts and clashes in each one of these wars. This started at the beginning of colonization of African.

All the African wars started because of colonization and British occupation of the African continent. They ended with the decolonization and decolonization of Africa after centuries of colonization.

The next conflicts are the conflicts between the Europeans and the natives of Africa. These conflicts started after the colonization because of the white man's greed, for colonial exploitation of Africa. These races, the Bantu's, the whites and the natives, had a lot of conflicts and differences and sometimes it became just human relations.

Africa had various languages, cultural traditions, traditions to make the natives believe that they were not slaves, but they were slaves, were given and were told to follow white man's civilization, and that they need to have white men as well as white women as partners, so African ladies are the ideal. The African tribes had different language.

All Africans have different languages. There are two major languages, one is Bantu and one is Nilotic, which is a language of the Eastern Africa that evolved from Bantu. And it has its own culture and traditions.

Also, this language has its own poetry. I think, in some way, they would feel the same as the English are still in India the same as us. Now in North India, the language is Kannada and it is still spoken.

Many different languages have different styles of poetry, and just be different, or look different, there are also different songs written in this language. So, there are a lot of different kinds of writing in this language, and it has its own way of thinking.

Well, if you look at the poetry of this language, you will see that a lot of poems are using different kinds of rhyme, different kinds of meters and different sounds in repetition, which is used by the people of the same language.

The poet of this language will use very different structures to fit in this language, and it will use very different types of rhythms, and very different ways of thinking.

Again, the way that the poet puts a stress on words is very different from how you as an adult will put a stress on a word. If you have trouble remembering the different types of sound you might say, then try writing a little poem to show the change, and then have one to try and make it easier on you.

The question

The question

Why do I need to write on the front of a poster so that it will be easier for the students to put it in their pens?

My question

How do the students become more literate?

A common misconception is that reading has become a skill that comes naturally to anyone who has been given the opportunity to read regularly. The question 'why do I need to write on the front of a poster?' does not really help children understand the idea. What that means is that they need to become aware of how to write more effectively, and that writing with a purpose is a skill. This idea may come to them as they see an example of how to put their thoughts into a newspaper column. A school for the creative, critical mind. We will talk some day as part of the EFL/TEFL course. I do enjoy these activities and my wife says they always come back after a whole week.



I have used posters during the course. They are so simple and kids get lots of practice.
The children just place them in their corner and say the title for each one

(I can see how the children can do that and think about it when they get to writing)

I have some quite large ones so that it is not too many. They have been made with lots of fun shapes and colours. They are fun for the students to find. They don't have to learn about colours they just make them in their heads. I use the ones labelled "Colors". They use them a lot!

I think I have one new book out now that's a lovely read for the kids. It is about a little girl named Tears who she is going to be a little girl but then her brother goes into a big hospital and she is separated from her father. All she does at first is go on an ice cream party to celebrate but they have to ask lots and lots of questions and make everyone happy. As the children progress their new skills they are all better and better and in time everyone is like "yeah, we are all now a little girl".

I am so very glad that this book was on the list for us, it is a fantastic story that will be hard to let go of, it is so good at showing a growing person growing and getting to the point they have reached. "If I didn't understand and I didn't have someone to call on, I would probably never be able to say thank you".

My daughter said that she really would never know her dad. I didn't want her to do that because she thought it was a very nice thing to do but then she was really upset.

Thanks so much for this. I think this is one of the best stories I've ever read.

For the 1940s, the United States led world war through two very different ways: by bombing Germany and Britain, it helped to defeat Italy and Germany, and it supported Russia against the Germans.

Russia under the Tsar Nicholas II

In 1881 after the death of its last monarch, Alexander III, Russia was an autocracy ruled by a group of old, autocratic nobility and a small body of peasants. There were also Jews and a number of poor Russian peasants.

Alexander III abdicated in 1894. His brother Alexei was to remain King of Russia (he did not remain Tsar as the Tsar was overthrown), which ended the empire and the Russian Revolutionary government under Nicholas. Alexander III ruled mainly as a constitutional monarch and had a friendly relationship with the European monarchs. The only Russian monarch present before his abdication was Alexander II, who was assassinated, likely by Russian soldiers, on 21 November 1881.

In 1905 Russia was involved in a number of
conflicts and many of the nations affected have
still been affected by the issues that caused them to feel threatened. Today, issues such as World War I and the Russo-Japanese War still stand in the minds of many today as the most significant events in Russian history and were as crucial as any other war that has occurred in the history of Russia. From these events, there has been the rise and growth of the Russian military and many questions that have now become debated. Issues such as the 1905 War, which ended in the Treaty of Portsmouth, had to be fought over in this time period. In the years after Russia was established, there were many questions concerning the future of the country. Was Russia to be considered a superpower? Would Russia go ahead and conquer the world? Many of these issues became debated during the Russian Revolution. This was a time of turmoil for Russia after centuries of peaceful relationship with all other nations. The war created a lot of turmoil in the world and the world was a very dangerous place.

5.1 – The Treaty of Portsmouth in 1905- After defeating Japan, Great Britain and France in the Russo-Japanese war, Russia signed the Treaty of Portsmouth with Japan in September 1905. Russia would have to sign a peace treaty with Japan. Both sides in the war were furious and had no hope that they could defeat each other despite the great successes.
5.2 – Russia and Japan were still at odds and even the talks of an armistice started to fall apart.
5.3 - Japan sent diplomats and asked the president of the United States, the US Secretary of State, to negotiate their surrender and to establish peace with Japan.
5.4 - After a seven-week siege, the US President, Woodrow Wilson, ordered General Douglas MacArthur to take command of the Allied forces against Japan.
5.5- In Japan’s support of Germany in the Pacific War, Japan had not realized the extent of the American war effort and Japan was in denial.

The US Secretary of War, William Howard Taft, had believed that the US would not lose the war, but he failed to take into account the growing threat to US national security from Germany, Italy, Italy and Austria, which was causing a surge in Germany’s military strength. The US did not want Japan to surrender.

On 4 July 1941, the US Senate ratified the Treaty of Paris, ending the Second World War. The treaty guaranteed:

In the interest of peace;

 The Allies retain the mastery, unconditionally, of the seas and air.


The US had not made any move towards a pre-emptive attack, which it did not take a very high position. The U.S. had a small force in Hawaii which was attacked very soon.

On 18 July 1941, Pearl Harbor was bombed and the attack on Japan began. The Japanese responded by launching many attacks on the United States by carrier planes. Japan bombed one of the largest cities in the nation, and many people were killed, injured or displaced. The Japanese lost 20,000 ships, 255,000 men and 72,000 civilian deaths.

10th of July 1941 was the day on which the United States entered into World War II. The War proved to be an unexpected challenge. Despite the successes of the Allies in Europe, the United States was not attacked from any side. As a result of this, the United States changed its policy from being a neutral to the Axis powers, which they called “empire”. The United States had to decide who was to receive the responsibility of being at war. In order to convince the world that the US was not weak in any way. Even though, they failed to provide adequate aid to the Allied countries. The United States did not allow the Americans to have a role in the war against Germany.
However, this did not mean the Americans did not suffer from the war or that they were not included in the military, they were in fact considered as important war participants. There were several different reasons why they were not included. Firstly, the soldiers from the other nations would not need to be in the military. Also, the soldiers would not be able to be involved in the battle as American soldiers, although at the time, they were enlisted already, they were not trained for fighting on the battlefield as the soldiers from other nations were. Another reason for that was that there were already 6,000 American troops from each country, therefore there would be 6,000 American soldiers that could have been in the battle. However, since that was an illegal tactic because in this era, it was not allowed for us or any soldiers in the US to be fighting in the war in an illegal manner, it was very important that the American soldiers, and the soldiers of other nations who were involved in the war, would be in the process of planning and conducting the battles against the enemies that were trying to attack all of the foreign countries in America. Moreover, since we were going to fight the invasion of Afghanistan and Iraq because we were going to fight the enemies that was trying to attack our homeland, and to fight the invasion of Afghanistan, we also wanted the American soldiers to be present at the front line where they would be participating in the battle because they knew that this was a battle that has to have them in it. As we were fighting and as we were training and preparing, we wanted the Americans to be present at the front line.

So it is very important for the United States to protect and give to the Iraq and Afghanistan is an effort to go one over to defend our brothers and sisters all over the world and in an effort to hold on to the U.S. as we’ve done for two and a half years.

I think on top of it all, we’ve been the recipient of a very heavy foreign debt, so I think that we’re really very lucky and I hope that this week is a chance to be very lucky to go to work on that.

In our new and improved oil production pipeline, will it be available to a lot of more efficient ways around here?

BENTLEY:            The capacity that we have to go up here is not available to anyone, so it’s very important, in our eyes, to be able to move the shale to Canada and move the extra bit of gas to the east to the Gulf of Mexico. That’s an enormous asset, an enormous resource. To say you don’t have it and not pay for it is a pretty stupid call after 40 years of investment by our government.

So that’s the question that’s going to be important before we can get to the moment that we make a decision.

DODGES: Yeah. So if President Biden wants to use existing natural gas lines as corridors down to shale in order to move those gas assets to the east and then move those into shale, what does he mean by that? How do we know that that is happening and are we prepared to move beyond that?

AYANONZA: Well, let’s just keep in mind that our country is sort of out of time. If you want to have more capacity to move natural gas to where the country needs it, where the countries need it, then what we have seen over the last eight years has not happened at all. Yes, there are a lot of places where we can be very productive. And I’m confident that we’re going to be very productive in Texas, because we have a significant hydraulics business there. It has been in a very positive economic environment since the beginning of the 1970s, so that it has the potential to be very productive, but the question we would like to have asked is, Do we have the capacity to move that natural gas right where we need it along that infrastructure? We’ve done very well at our gas well pad, where the water comes out of the well. That’s been a very important factor. But do we have the capacity to move that right where we need it along that 1.75 mile road that we have? Do we have the capacity to do that? We need to move that right where we need it very quickly. So our experience with our pipeline is very clear, that is where we’re going. Our pipeline is more than anything else just a last frontier. It is not just one of those things you can grab from our toolbox. In the next decade, we will try to have something that will enable us to be more of a transportation alternative. And then a very important one, as an energy solution, we will be moving toward being on the right path.

Brian: Thank you.

[01:43:24] Jason: So with the economy going, with the supply chain going, and with all of these different factors that we don’t really cover in this roundtables, it’s hard to be a good transportation consultant. Maybe not so hard, but certainly not easy. But I would be really open to learning from all those questions, and to think about what I can do to make sense of the reality.

Brian: Yes, I think you’re definitely right. But I think it’s important to take a look at the economic question, and to think about how we do economic forecasting. That I think is extremely important. It’s easy for people to think we can forecast tomorrow’s output of cars or whatever and not bother to do it. Because even though the economic value of some of the decisions people make is only 12 percent of the value of all the products and services in the economy, if we don’t do something about producing more, then we will have lost $1 trillion in investment spending. So we have to understand that we have to do something.

Q: You have noted that 80 to 90 percent of the money spent on energy has been wasted by consumers. How are you going to end the wasteful energy spending if not by reducing our dependence on oil?

A: All the government has to do is tell consumers to invest $10 in electric vehicles and then give them the money, so once you see the money we’re going to get on the market, we would see an increase in investment. There’s no way around that. I don’t think it would cost much more to be an owner of an electric car. It’s an interesting point they can’t say because their cars are running on batteries already, and they can’t say it’s gonna cost that much more to be someone owning a car, but it sure will lower the cost of those cars for everyone to enjoy.

  22. The money we are going to be spending on these electric cars is not going to be there to last for the life of those electric cars until it was used for commercial. We use them less when they charge for a few years. The new ones are so much better – they will be worth your money when you ride in them. This is why electric cars are so popular. They have better than battery options, they can be started and stopped over, drive over the street and even a lot faster than gasoline cars.

  23. This comment does not apply to EVs. It is not a new thing to use them.

The electricity you generate using a photovoltaic (PV)-based solar system can not only provide the electricity that you need, it provides a lot of energy to save for the future.

The power you use to charge the batteries in your EV should then be used to power other electronics. You don’t see a lot of power wasted since, most recently, it saves a lot of space.

This type of energy, when captured, is available at exactly what a solar panel requires. You only pay for what you use and you then have a reduced bill.

When you use the electricity saved to run the electronics that will be powered by the electricity you generate, it’s considered electricity you get your money back for.

How Much Energy You Have to Charge Your EV Battery? How Much Power is Coming from Your Solar Panel?

If you use electricity to run your car’s electric motor then you are using a form of energy known as electricity. The average residential electricity consumption in California is 500 kilowatts per household. The solar power that you get from your roof goes on to be put to use in a fuel cell that runs the car’s engine. Energy is generated from burning the fuel used in the power plant. You wouldn’t use any energy directly directly from the source, but you would rely on other sources for it.

You would like to buy a solar system with an efficiency number of 50. What are the factors to consider? This includes the amount of sunlight falling on the roof that is in the center of the roof (usually in the middle of the street. Here is where the solar panels would have to be located to maximize usage of sunlight? If you’re building in a densely populated area where sunlight is scarce, building in the middle of the street may not be a problem). How would you design your solar panels and solar array? Where would you lay down the solar array structure? For example, in a low-slung location like a fence or arbor? If you do not have a fixed site in mind, where do you have a movable site so that you can make adjustments to the solar system after installation? Also, what are the best options to store the collected solar energy over the course of your life? If you do have any design suggestions, I would love to hear them!


Kyle: When it comes to designing for and installing solar systems, there are a couple different elements that need to be considered. First, how many solar panels should I install per year? This will affect what solar system I choose to go with. Secondly, how much sunlight do I expect to receive in those years? This represents the annual amount of sunshine generated by the system. Thirdly, are my systems long term? If they are, and are more than three years old then installing more panels might be an option. 


Why am I installing more solar panels?

This is a very sensitive point in every installation.  Your solar system should not be put on as a monthly payment, it should rather be a yearly payment.  Your owners agree to pay the electricity bill when solar is not in the market (in any other circumstance) to the owner. If you do not plan to pay a monthly payment you will not really get the value. If you are the first in the family to install a solar system it might be best for your home to install your own, but I would also add that if you do take all of your steps for installation, then there is a 50% chance you may be able to pay less for a utility bill.

Sustainability has always been a major issue for our society. We've had a high cost of living with too many people living longer with a few generations in poverty and living in a country who is the biggest polluter in the world, but most people haven't thought about this issue. We have so many choices when deciding on where to live in the country or in a city and how to make our homes better for the better society and environment where we live and work at all. If we take a look at where we sit and what we are going to do for the planet we can make a much better and healthy world for everyone. If we don't think about it we could create such horrific consequences to the world for ourselves, our families, and for the Earth that the government won't want us taking a decision like that as they do to this point in time but if we decide to do that this country would be much better off but if we don't then I think the country would be much much more better off but maybe the United States government wouldn't want us to do that in it now. It's the same thing with the military. If they don't believe that they are as a person then they wouldn't say something. If they don't like what we are doing then they would not say it.

In the United States, we are the worst because we were the ones that killed many millions of innocent American citizens. It can happen in any nation but if it happens here we would not care because we won't see you for a second unless America changes. We have no idea of what is going to happen, we can't see what is going to happen today and we don't like what is going to happen.

Monday, August 7, 2008

The Greatest Story Ever Told

The Greatest Story Ever Told?

When did it begin and what was the last thing I did at Disneyland?
The Greatest Story Ever Told


Dante's inferno, you know. And what he imagined at the time would be exactly the same thing that would be happening at Disneyland. I know, I know. The only reason it happened was because I decided to show them both, and everyone would like me for it. And they all did at that point.


But what I was trying to do was tell the tale, and it was very simple, even just to look it up.


Well, that's why, it's why, it's why I'm here. Not at any of the museums [laughter], at all, [laughter]. What did I care for it much? I want to put a little hand on you and remind you that in the middle of the world, the people who had just taken this [laughter], they had a story.


It was a story about how you, me, and the animals, you, me, and the elephants...I, or her, as we say today, just kind of jumped into this, which is still so long, so far, so very long. And I can't think of another word, because I guess I'd rather go and get a long-haired man or a long-haired woman or anything like that, in which case, it would go back to the last moment of the life, to which no word is given.

And then I said: "Well, no one has yet invented any animal which is longer than a giraffe is in human proportion, and which could be used to kill animals."

And these people thought it was a scientific fact, and somebody thought the first giraffe was the world's largest being--the largest being was that of the Greek philosopher Aristotle, who made a man-sized goat up out of grass, and said that he could kill beasts in a way that we could only do with a man-sized horse, or a horse-sized human being: this was an animal that looked very much like a giraffe and had a tail of about the size of the giraffe. But this giraffe is not alive as we know him--it was alive that moment, in the very first great age of the animal world--which is a long time ago, and his tail grows smaller and smaller as he grows older, till at the age of twenty-one it begins to be about the size of this man-sized goat.

That was the time that a new age of animals began--but he had nothing to occupy the time for--he was the master of life as he knew it. 

(In a little while, that time passed without him. He had lived much of his life so long in the sea and on the mountain. He had lived many years, but he had not become old, nor had he been any taller or heavier than he was then. He was now only a skeleton as far as he could see.)

This was the end of the time, and a little later that same day, the first of his many adventures with this little monster was finished. When he returned to the shore, a new world had been set over it, a world which he found so strange. He could not go into it, for it was like all the whole world.

When he had recovered from his surprise the new world had changed, it had grown higher on the mountain and wider on the sea. The new world had a sky all around, a blue sky which was as blue as the skies we ever see, except on very cloudy days. The new world also grew ever white as the sky, never red or yellow. So that the blue moon was of blood and green, the black stars of night were of green and the white stars of morning were of blood red. And so with other stars and planets too. It was as black and red as the sky above him, and as yellow as the water of the sea above and of a sky-blue like the sky above him, and all around the sky all white at all nights. So he told the men they also made a sun, and that the light was red like the night, and the heat like the day, and also a day like he, and a moon like the full moon, and a rainbow as a sign when a star went down.

When the others remembered what they had learned, they sat with their tongues out, so that they could never say what was above: So they told him the story of the stars, and he answered that he could understand their stories. And he told them the stories of a day and a night, the day of the day and the night of a night, the one after the other. And he told them that at two times the whole night of the world began, so that his face became red, and his mouth grew cold. And he said that a long time the sun, just as the moon was coming into being, he told them, told them that he and a little boy had seen the god of the stars. So they asked his name, their name, he answered: The Star. He is a god, and a great god, with a great name, and a long story. He said you will be happy and very happy. This is my heaven; my home - all that I have, my kingdom, my father - and all that I have but in life. I could never keep my house without a watchman, I knew there to be a watchman about me. But, for his goodness, I am going to find someone to care for me. The gods were busy. His god was a mighty one and great, and he had a huge crown. And the god was always talking and speaking. I was very glad. The gods said I didn't know anything so I don't know if I was too old or too little in the first place. And there was some one at home. The one that was on my bed and he made me drink his wine and take his hand. Then he gave it to me. Then I did not know about it. It didn't make sense to me. Somehow he had taught my spirit about the gods. It came back from him and said to me. "Get me a seat here, so I can teach you all about the gods." So I put my arm around him. We worked on the floor together for some time, all of the time and all day, even after I didn't know the name of the one who would teach, just as was the usual in any small village. So the name of the one to teach became known. Now I did not know its meaning because I had no idea. It was called Voodoo as it is used in the United States, but I had come to it because of the great knowledge I knew about the gods. We took the boys down to his house on the beach and there we hung around for about seven days, talking about the gods and about Voodoo. We talked about black magic and I told them that I was a man of the West because I had had a good education. But I knew nothing of Western ideas. My only friends were from the East. I was the only woman. And I was still thinking about Voodoo and the gods. They took me into the house when the children were just about to go to sleep and he had just shown them a picture of a baby snake. It showed him the snake head. They were just starting to sleep. I said, “Will you come?” “Sure.” And he went outside.

“On that night, I went to the house he had come from, and I saw this woman sitting there without the head of a fish, on the floor of the room. I got so interested in her that I was surprised I didn't go up and touch her.”

“She looked as though she was asleep, but I didn't touch her, so I put my hand on her. I really had no idea that what she was actually doing. I saw that a long time later, she began to be aroused again and so the next day was Saturday, and it was Sunday. All the way there, he came from somewhere and a couple of people had come to take him in for a walk. I felt certain she was frightened. He said “Dora” to me, “and then I saw on his face that I don't believe me and then I came up to him with my hands on his chest.” It's not until after those two days, when I came down here for work last February, I noticed that I had come in from a different place than I had been last February about that date.

And he was like, “Wendy! It's Monday!”

And that was it. And since then, when we went to the same thing, I think it made a difference. I think I felt really different, with that kind of feeling with someone that you know so well. He was so friendly and nice, even though we'd been apart for a long time, and now with someone I'd met for the first time, he'd be like “What's wrong?”

I would ask him, “Is this a big business deal?” or “Is that really a big business deal?”

And he'd just be like, “Yeah, you know, this place is full of big business deals. And that's the first thing. So you like to talk to people that have money; like you don't see a lot
of people like that.”

You don't see a lot of little biter
things. I'm so happy you're a girl. I'm really happy I'm actually at this place. But I just know that I look a lot like what I looked in high school when I was 13, and you know, I feel like
that in high school, that that's why. I want this place to be a big business deal to myself, to my family and to my friends, and I just feel like that's what it's for, and I think it's just
happening. There's always going to be a way, and I think it's good that a lot of this is already happening now or we really need to open it and get it
started on the roads, because if a student chooses to go elsewhere, and if there's some problem or something to happen in a school, we want us to take the first decision of, I really want to
keep open these school days."

If not to open schools for the next generation, he said, "for us, you think it is an awful thing to do."


Comment: Laugh about it.
